dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 8675 IH: Training Rural Law Enforcement Officers Act of 2026
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mrs. Bice (for herself and Ms. Kaptur) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on the Judiciary

form.official-title: To authorize the Department of Justice to provide grant funding to accredited nonprofit organizations to provide better access to needed training for law enforcement officers in rural and smaller communities.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Training Rural Law Enforcement Officers Act of 2026.

paragraph.text: There are more than 18,000 local police departments and 3,000 sheriff’s offices in the United States. Of the local police departments in the United States—

subparagraph.text: nearly half have fewer than 10 sworn officers;
3 out of 4 have fewer than 2 dozen sworn officers; and
8 out of 10 have fewer than 50 sworn officers.

paragraph.text: Federal funding available through grants administered by the Department of Justice for law enforcement is frequently complex in terms of the application process and requires specific reporting mandates.

Smaller and rural law enforcement agencies often forego pursuing Federal funding opportunities as that pursuit creates a demand on already limited staffing, and those smaller and rural law enforcement agencies simply lack the experience and resources to navigate through the application process and to successfully adhere to the multitude of reporting requirements.
The term accredited nonprofit organization means a nonprofit organization that, as determined by the Attorney General, has—

subparagraph.text: the proper experience and expertise in relevant law enforcement training; and
a strong track record of successfully conducting relevant law enforcement training in a particular training discipline.

paragraph.text: The term law enforcement training grant means a grant awarded by the Attorney General under the Omnibus Crime Control and Safe Streets Act of 1968 (34 U.S.C. 10101 et seq.) for the purpose of law enforcement training.

subsection.text: An accredited nonprofit organization shall be eligible to receive any law enforcement training grant for the purpose of providing training to State and local law enforcement agencies if the training is—

paragraph.text: consistent with the priorities and objectives of the Department of Justice;
provided to a law enforcement agency with fewer than 50 sworn law enforcement officers; and
conducted at no cost to the law enforcement agency receiving the training.
